codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly enforces required note/reason for period close and reopen actions, and fixes the period state machine so LOCKED periods can show both Close and Reopen buttons per the backend contract. Test coverage is thorough with 9 new tests covering lock button visibility, required validation gates, cascade reverse, non-POSTED guards, and reload-after-reverse. The code follows existing patterns and maintains consistency with the codebase.

issues.description: The confirm button uses both 'disabled' attribute and opacity class. While functional, the visual opacity cue alone doesn't provide explicit error feedback to users. Consider adding an error hint below the input field when the required field is empty, similar to how other forms in the app handle validation errors.

The test 'opens cascade reverse confirmation modal on Cascade Reverse click' verifies the modal opens but does not test the actual cascade reverse API call. Consider adding a test that verifies cascadeReverseJournal is called with the correct parameters when the modal is confirmed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The required field validation pattern (confirm button disabled + opacity visual) is used consistently here but not documented in AGENTS.md. Other forms in the codebase use similar patterns with error messages below inputs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AccountingPeriodsPage.tsx:248 — disabled={isLoading || isNoteInvalid} with opacity class

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend period state machine (LOCKED -> OPEN via reopen, LOCKED -> CLOSED via close) was discovered by the worker through backend handoff review. This knowledge could be useful for future period-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker grep commands in transcript searching frontend-handoff.md for period-related DTOs

summary: PASS. The worker correctly implemented the required note/reason enforcement for period close/reopen and fixed the LOCKED period state machine. The 9 new tests provide solid coverage of the key behaviors. Two minor suggestions noted for enhanced UX feedback and additional test coverage for cascade reverse API calls, but neither is blocking. The implementation follows project conventions and the skill procedure was followed correctly.
